---
title: "Bayesian Project Proposal Template"
---

## Project Title

State a concise title that names the scientific problem rather than only the model class.

## Scientific Question

- What is the substantive question?
- What is the target estimand?
- Why is a Bayesian approach appropriate?

## Data And Measurement Context

- What are the observational units?
- How were the data collected?
- What dependence, grouping, missingness, or measurement issues are expected?

## Candidate Model Family

Write the generative structure:

$$
p(y,\theta)=p(y \mid \theta)\pi(\theta).
$$

If hierarchical or latent structure is involved, write the factorization in expanded form.

## Prior Strategy

- Which priors will be used?
- What scale information justifies them?
- What prior predictive checks will be run?

## Computational Plan

- Which inference method will be used?
- Why is it appropriate for the model geometry?
- Which diagnostics will be used?

## Model Ladder

List:

1. the baseline model;
2. the reference model;
3. at least one sensitivity or robustness alternative.

## Evaluation Plan

- How will predictive performance be assessed?
- How will sensitivity analysis be conducted?
- What constitutes success for the project?

## Risks

List the main expected failure modes:

- identifiability;
- poor mixing or divergences;
- prior sensitivity;
- weak signal;
- data quality problems.

## Deliverables

- proposal;
- reproducible code;
- diagnostics;
- final report;
- appendix of sensitivity analyses.
